commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4247f0ae42ac626ef3e13fbda5d1c0549f952f43)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes both Feishu `createFeishuReplyDispatcher` call sites to pass computed `feishuTo` as the dispatcher's `chatId` instead of raw inbound `ctx.chatId`.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked failure is source-reproducible: current main computes `user:<open_id>` for Feishu DMs but passes raw `ctx.chatId` to the dispatcher while reply metadata can be suppressed; I did not run a live Feishu tenant.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the branch has a message-delivery correctness finding and the contributor still needs live Feishu proof; automation cannot supply the external Feishu environment evidence.

target.title: fix(feishu): route p2p DM replies to user open_id instead of p2p group chat_id (fixes #83730)
target.body:
## What

Route p2p DM replies to the sender's `open_id` instead of the p2p group's `chat_id`.

When the Feishu bot receives a p2p (DM) message, the incoming `ctx.chatId` is the p2p group's `oc_xxx` identifier. The reply dispatcher was receiving this `oc_xxx` ID as its `chatId`, causing it to send replies via `im.message.create` with `receive_id_type=chat_id` targeting the p2p group. In WebSocket mode, this fails with `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` because the bot's WebSocket subscription does not cover the p2p group through the Feishu API subscription model.

The fix passes `feishuTo` (which resolves to `user:ou_xxx` for p2p chats) instead of `ctx.chatId` to `createFeishuReplyDispatcher` in both the broadcast and main dispatch paths. This routes replies through `im.message.create` with `receive_id_type=open_id`, which correctly delivers to the user.

## Why

- `feishuTo` is already computed at `bot.ts:853` as `isGroup ? 'chat:${ctx.chatId}' : 'user:${ctx.senderOpenId}'`
- The reply dispatcher uses `chatId` as the send target for all non-reply messages (streaming, final text, cards)
- For p2p chats, `ctx.chatId` is `oc_xxx` (the p2p group), but replies should target `user:ou_xxx` (the user)
- `skipReplyToInMessages` already handles skipping the reply-to for p2p, but the send target was still wrong

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts`: Pass `feishuTo` instead of `ctx.chatId` to `createFeishuReplyDispatcher` in both dispatch paths (broadcast at ~line 1601, main at ~line 1779)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Feishu p2p DM replies fail with SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND when using WebSocket mode
- **Environment tested:** macOS, OpenClaw main branch (6ca856ba), Feishu extension
- **Steps run after the patch:** Verified `feishuTo` resolves to `user:ou_xxx` for p2p chats by tracing `bot.ts:853`. Confirmed `resolveReceiveIdType("user:ou_xxx")` returns `"open_id"` via `targets.ts:70-72`. Ran all Feishu extension tests (bot: 87, reply-dispatcher: 82, channel: 61 — all pass).
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts
 ✓  extension-feishu  extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts (87 tests) 79ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  87 passed (87)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts
 ✓  extension-feishu  extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts (82 tests) 38ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  82 passed (82)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts
 ✓  extension-feishu  extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts (61 tests) 13ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  61 passed (61)

$ grep -n "chatId: feishuTo" extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts
1601:              chatId: feishuTo,
1779:          chatId: feishuTo,
```
- **Observed result after fix:** All 230 Feishu extension tests pass. The reply dispatcher now receives `user:ou_xxx` for p2p chats, which resolves to `receive_id_type=open_id` for message delivery.
- **What was not tested:** Live Feishu WebSocket p2p interaction (requires Feishu app credentials and WebSocket connection).
